REMARK: AS TO THE MAY-23-2024 EX PARTE HEARING ON DEFENDANT ARNELLE CHINDIA BERRY'S APPLICATION FOR AN ORDER SHORTENING TIME RE: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND REQUEST FOR COURT JUDGMENT, THE COURT DENIES THE DEFENDANT'S APPLICATION. THE DEFENDANT MAY BRING THEIR REQUEST AS A REGULARLY-NOTICED MOTION. THE COURT WILL PERMIT THE DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE WHICH STATES THAT THE PROVE-UP HEARING IS TO BE STAYED UNTIL THE COURT RULES ON DEFENDANT'S REGULARLY-NOTICED MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)
